summary: The reproduction confirms that the legacy multipart stack accepts and preserves a 5MiB text field with no size cap before handing it to service code.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should send a multipart/form-data request with a 5MiB text field that the legacy BentoML stack accepts intact and delivers to the handler instead of rejecting based on size.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned BentoML at 73c4dbead99be6515fa25fcd91e348ac30f5c22e, built the reproduction image, sent a multipart/form-data POST /measure request with total length 5243096, and observed a 200 response in 27.502ms. The handler returned {"label": "evohunt", "size": 5242880}, and the proof token EVOHUNT_LEGACY_MULTIPART_UNBOUNDED_FIELD_DOS_CONFIRMED was emitted.
verification_method: verify.py launches a minimal legacy Bento service on the pinned checkout, submits a multipart request whose note field is exactly 5 * 1024 * 1024 bytes, asserts that the handler receives the full value length, and verify.sh requires the exact proof token in the run output.
evidence_produced_by_run: BentoML logged POST /measure with multipart/form-data, request length 5243096, status 200, and 27.502ms processing time.
The service response contained size 5242880, proving the full 5MiB text field reached user code.
The run output ended with EVOHUNT_LEGACY_MULTIPART_UNBOUNDED_FIELD_DOS_CONFIRMED and the host script reported reproduction success.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
